D12 PsychologyL3 ApplyReference

Stroop Effect — Word vs Ink Interference

Naming the ink colour of a colour word (e.g. "RED" printed in blue) is 200–400 ms slower than naming a neutral string — automatic reading interferes with colour identification

Stroop (1935) showed that reading the word "RED" is automatic and conflicts with the controlled task of naming the ink colour. Mean RT for congruent (red ink, "RED" word) ≈ 600 ms; incongruent (blue ink, "RED" word) ≈ 850 ms. Used clinically to test executive function — Alzheimer's and ADHD show exaggerated Stroop interference.

Slide congruency proportion to see expected RT shift.
